stories.title: 回来的客人
stories.source_range: inspired by early repeat-customer and seller-growth scenes

sentences.chinese: 有一天，杂草正在市场边上整理自己的东西。
sentences.english_helper: One day, Weed is organizing his things at the side of the market.

sentences.chinese: 一个人停在他面前。
sentences.english_helper: A person stops in front of him.

sentences.chinese: 杂草抬头一看，觉得这个人有一点眼熟。
sentences.english_helper: Weed looks up and thinks the person seems a little familiar.

sentences.chinese: 后来他才想起来。
sentences.english_helper: Then he finally remembers.

sentences.chinese: 这个人以前买过他一个小雕刻。
sentences.english_helper: This person bought one of his small carvings before.

sentences.chinese: 那时候，杂草只觉得自己运气不错。
sentences.english_helper: At that time, Weed only thought he had gotten lucky.

sentences.chinese: 买完以后，客人就走了。
sentences.english_helper: After buying it, the customer left.

sentences.chinese: 所以杂草没有再多想。
sentences.english_helper: So Weed did not think more about it.

sentences.chinese: 可是现在，这个人又回头了。
sentences.english_helper: But now, this person has come back.

sentences.chinese: 客人先看了看摊上的东西。
sentences.english_helper: The customer first looks over the pieces on display.

sentences.chinese: 然后说：“你最近做得比以前更好了。”
sentences.english_helper: Then says, 'What you've made lately is better than before.'

sentences.chinese: 杂草听见这句话，心里先是一愣。
sentences.english_helper: When Weed hears that sentence, he is startled first.

sentences.chinese: 因为他没有想到，对方会记得。
sentences.english_helper: Because he did not expect the other person to remember.

sentences.chinese: 在他心里，卖掉就是卖掉了。
sentences.english_helper: In his mind, once something is sold, it is simply sold.

sentences.chinese: 东西离手，事情也就结束。
sentences.english_helper: Once the item leaves his hand, the matter is over.

sentences.chinese: 可是这个客人不是这样。
sentences.english_helper: But this customer is not like that.

sentences.chinese: 客人又说：“上次那个我朋友很喜欢。”
sentences.english_helper: The customer adds, 'My friend liked the one from last time very much.'

sentences.chinese: “所以我想再看看。”
sentences.english_helper: 'So I wanted to come look again.'

sentences.chinese: 杂草这一次没有马上报价格。
sentences.english_helper: This time, Weed does not quote a price right away.

sentences.chinese: 他先把两件比较像的作品拿出来。
sentences.english_helper: He first brings out two pieces that are somewhat similar.

sentences.chinese: 又说了它们不一样的地方。
sentences.english_helper: Then he explains how they are different.

sentences.chinese: 客人听得很认真。
sentences.english_helper: The customer listens very carefully.

sentences.chinese: 最后选了一件新的。
sentences.english_helper: In the end, the customer chooses a new one.

sentences.chinese: 付钱的时候，客人笑着说：“以后我还会来。”
sentences.english_helper: While paying, the customer smiles and says, 'I'll come again later.'

sentences.chinese: 客人走了以后，杂草还站在那里。
sentences.english_helper: After the customer leaves, Weed is still standing there.

sentences.chinese: 市场照样很吵。
sentences.english_helper: The market is still noisy as usual.

sentences.chinese: 可是他心里有一种新的感觉。
sentences.english_helper: But there is a new feeling in his heart.

sentences.chinese: 原来有人不是只买一次。
sentences.english_helper: So there are people who do not buy only once.

sentences.chinese: 原来做得认真一点，别人真的会记得。
sentences.english_helper: So if he works a little more seriously, people really will remember.

sentences.chinese: 杂草终于明白，卖东西不只是把东西推出去。
sentences.english_helper: Weed finally understands that selling something is not only about pushing the item out.

sentences.chinese: 也是让别人开始信你。
sentences.english_helper: It is also about making others begin to trust you.

stories.title: 灯还亮着的晚上
stories.source_range: inspired by late-night work and fatigue-management scenes

new_words.gloss: to push through; to force oneself on

sentences.chinese: 那天晚上，杂草的房间里很安静。
sentences.english_helper: That night, Weed's room is very quiet.

sentences.chinese: 只有一盏小台灯还亮着。
sentences.english_helper: Only one small desk lamp is still on.

sentences.chinese: 桌上放着木头、刀，还有没做完的小人。
sentences.english_helper: On the table are wood, a knife, and a half-finished little figure.

sentences.chinese: 杂草已经做了很久。
sentences.english_helper: Weed has already been working for a long time.

sentences.chinese: 眼睛有一点酸。
sentences.english_helper: His eyes ache a little.

sentences.chinese: 手指也开始发抖。
sentences.english_helper: His fingers also begin to shake.

sentences.chinese: 其实他自己知道，应该先休息一下。
sentences.english_helper: Actually, he knows himself that he should rest for a bit first.

sentences.chinese: 可是他没有停。
sentences.english_helper: But he does not stop.

sentences.chinese: 因为他一直有一个想法。
sentences.english_helper: Because he has always had one thought.

sentences.chinese: 休息就是落后。
sentences.english_helper: Rest means falling behind.

sentences.chinese: 停下来，就是浪费。
sentences.english_helper: Stopping means wasting time.

sentences.chinese: 如果别人还在走，自己就不能坐。
sentences.english_helper: If others are still moving forward, he cannot sit still.

sentences.chinese: 所以他继续撑。
sentences.english_helper: So he keeps forcing himself onward.

sentences.chinese: 可是越撑，他的手越硬。
sentences.english_helper: But the more he pushes through, the stiffer his hand becomes.

sentences.chinese: 刀也越不听话。
sentences.english_helper: The knife also becomes harder to control.

sentences.chinese: 本来很简单的一刀，结果还是偏了。
sentences.english_helper: What should have been a simple cut still goes off.

sentences.chinese: 杂草盯着那道错开的线，心里一下子很烦。
sentences.english_helper: Weed stares at the crooked line and feels irritated at once.

sentences.chinese: 他差一点就想更快地补回去。
sentences.english_helper: He almost wants to fix it immediately and even faster.

sentences.chinese: 可是他没有马上动。
sentences.english_helper: But he does not move right away.

sentences.chinese: 他只是把刀放下。
sentences.english_helper: He only puts the knife down.

sentences.chinese: 又低头看了看自己的手。
sentences.english_helper: Then he lowers his head and looks at his own hand.

sentences.chinese: 那只手抖得比他想的还厉害。
sentences.english_helper: That hand is shaking more than he thought.

sentences.chinese: 这时候，他终于承认了一件事。
sentences.english_helper: At that moment, he finally admits one thing.

sentences.chinese: 自己现在不是在努力。
sentences.english_helper: Right now he is not being diligent.

sentences.chinese: 自己现在只是在跟累硬拼。
sentences.english_helper: Right now he is only fighting exhaustion head-on.

sentences.chinese: 杂草起身去洗了脸。
sentences.english_helper: Weed gets up and washes his face.

sentences.chinese: 又喝了一点水。
sentences.english_helper: Then he drinks a little water.

sentences.chinese: 他只休息了不长的时间。
sentences.english_helper: He rests only for a short time.

sentences.chinese: 可是回到桌前的时候，手已经稳了一些。
sentences.english_helper: But when he returns to the table, his hand is already steadier.

sentences.chinese: 后来那一刀，也终于做对了。
sentences.english_helper: Later, that cut is finally done correctly.

sentences.chinese: 台灯还亮着。
sentences.english_helper: The desk lamp is still on.

sentences.chinese: 可是杂草的想法已经有一点不一样了。
sentences.english_helper: But Weed's thinking is already a little different.

sentences.chinese: 他终于明白，真正想走远的人，也要知道什么时候先坐一下。
sentences.english_helper: He finally understands that someone who truly wants to go far must also know when to sit down for a moment first.
